channel.title: Cybersecurity News Aggregator
channel.description: Latest cybersecurity news from top sources
channel.comment: News aggregated from: Krebs on Security, The Hacker News, Threatpost, Bleeping Computer, Dark Reading

item.title: Critical vm2 sandbox bug lets attackers execute code on hosts
item.description: A critical vulnerability in the popular Node.js sandboxing library vm2 allows escaping the sandbox and executing arbitrary code on the host system. [...]...
item.dc:creator: Bleeping Computer

item.title: New Cisco DoS flaw requires manual reboot to revive devices
item.description: Cisco patched a Crosswork Network Controller and Network Services Orchestrator denial-of-service vulnerability that requires manually rebooting targeted systems for recovery. [...]...
item.dc:creator: Bleeping Computer

item.title: DAEMON Tools devs confirm breach, release malware-free version
item.description: Disc Soft Limited, the maker of DAEMON Tools Lite, confirmed that the software had been trojanized in a supply chain attack and released a new, malware-free version. [...]...
item.dc:creator: Bleeping Computer

item.title: Why ransomware attacks succeed even when backups exist
item.description: Backups don't fail because they're missing, they fail because attackers destroy them first. Acronis explains how ransomware targets backup systems before encryption, leaving no path to recovery. [...]...
item.dc:creator: Bleeping Computer

item.title: MuddyWater hackers use Chaos ransomware as a decoy in attacks
item.description: The MuddyWater Iranian hackers disguised their operations as a Chaos ransomware attack, relying on Microsoft Teams social engineering to gain access and establish persistence. [...]...
item.dc:creator: Bleeping Computer

item.title: MuddyWater Uses Microsoft Teams to Steal Credentials in False Flag Ransomware Attack
item.description: The Iranian state-sponsored hacking group known as MuddyWater (aka Mango Sandstorm, Seedworm, and Static Kitten) has been attributed to a ransomware attack in what has been described as a "false flag"...
item.dc:creator: The Hacker News

item.title: Webinar: Why network incidents escalate and how to fix response gaps
item.description: Most network incidents don't escalate due to a lack of alerts; they escalate when response breaks down. This webinar explores how to fix gaps in triage, enrichment, and coordination. [...]...
item.dc:creator: Bleeping Computer

item.title: The Hacker News Launches 'Cybersecurity Stars Awards 2026' — Submissions Now Open
item.description: For nearly 20 years, we at The Hacker News have mostly told scary stories about cyberspace — big hacks, broken systems, and new threats. But behind every headline, there’s a quieter, better story. It’...
item.dc:creator: The Hacker News

item.title: From Stuxnet to ChatGPT: 20 News Events That Shaped Cyber
item.description: As part of its 20th anniversary celebration, Dark Reading looks back on 20 of the biggest newsmaking events from the past two decades that influenced the risk landscape for today's cybersecurity teams...
item.dc:creator: Dark Reading

item.title: Your AI Agents Are Already Inside the Perimeter. Do You Know What They're Doing?
item.description: Analysts recently confirmed what identity security teams have quietly feared: AI agents are being deployed faster than enterprises can govern them. In their inaugural Market Guide for Guardian Agents,...
item.dc:creator: The Hacker News

item.title: Attacks Abuse Windows Phone Link to Steal Texts & Bypass 2FA
item.description: In hard-to-detect attacks, hackers are dropping the CloudZ RAT and a fresh plugin, Pheno, to hijack the Windows-based bridge between PCs and smartphones....
item.dc:creator: Dark Reading

item.title: Palo Alto Networks warns of firewall RCE zero-day exploited in attacks
item.description: Palo Alto Networks warned customers today that a critical-severity unpatched vulnerability in the PAN-OS User-ID Authentication Portal is being exploited in attacks. [...]...
item.dc:creator: Bleeping Computer

item.title: Google's Android Apps Get Public Verification to Stop Supply Chain Attacks
item.description: Google has announced expanded Binary Transparency for Android as a way to safeguard the ecosystem from supply chain attacks. "This new public ledger ensures the Google apps on your device are exactly ...
item.dc:creator: The Hacker News

item.title: Windows Phone Link Exploited by CloudZ RAT to Steal Credentials and OTPs
item.description: Cybersecurity researchers have disclosed details of an intrusion that involved the use of a CloudZ remote access tool (RAT) and a previous undocumented plugin dubbed Pheno with the aim of facilitating...
item.dc:creator: The Hacker News

item.title: Palo Alto PAN-OS Flaw Under Active Exploitation Enables Remote Code Execution
item.description: Palo Alto Networks has released an advisory warning that a critical buffer overflow vulnerability in its PAN-OS software has been exploited in the wild. The vulnerability, tracked as CVE-2026-0300, ha...
item.dc:creator: The Hacker News

item.title: Middle East Cyber Battle Field Broadens — Especially in UAE
item.description: As the war with Iran continues, breach attempts targeting the United Arab Emirates tripled in a few weeks — many targeting critical infrastructure....
item.dc:creator: Dark Reading

item.title: New stealthy Quasar Linux malware targets software developers
item.description: A previously undocumented Linux implant named Quasar Linux (QLNX) is targeting developers' systems with a mix of rootkit, backdoor, and credential-stealing capabilities. [...]...
item.dc:creator: Bleeping Computer

item.title: Instructure hacker claims data theft from 8,800 schools, universities
item.description: The hacker behind a breach at education technology giant Instructure claims to have stolen 280 million data records for students and staff from 8,809 colleges, school districts, and online education p...
item.dc:creator: Bleeping Computer

item.title: Trellix Source Code Breach Highlights Growing Supply Chain Threats
item.description: Info is scant, but such breaches can reveal where a security product's controls are located and how detections are designed, giving attackers a leg up....
item.dc:creator: Dark Reading

item.title: Research Hub Bridges Cybersecurity Gap for Under-Resourced Organizations
item.description: The UC Berkeley Center for Long-Term Cybersecurity (CLTC) offers tools and support to schools, local governments, and non-profits as they defend themselves against a growing volume of cyberattacks....
item.dc:creator: Dark Reading

item.title: DAEMON Tools trojanized in supply-chain attack to deploy backdoor
item.description: Hackers trojanized installers for the DAEMON Tools software and since April 8, delivered a backdoor to thousands of systems that downloaded the product from the official website. [...]...
item.dc:creator: Bleeping Computer

item.title: Why Security Leadership Makes or Breaks a Pen Test
item.description: Well-run security drills go beyond checking audit boxes to identify and address trouble spots. Effective leaders can ensure proper scope, access, and follow-through, but it’s not easy....
item.dc:creator: Dark Reading

item.title: Student hacked Taiwan high-speed rail to trigger emergency brakes
item.description: A 23-year-old university student in Taiwan was arrested for interfering with the TETRA communication system used by the country's high-speed railway network (THSR). [...]...
item.dc:creator: Bleeping Computer

item.title: Critical Apache HTTP/2 Flaw (CVE-2026-23918) Enables DoS and Potential RCE
item.description: The Apache Software Foundation (ASF) has released security updates to address several security vulnerabilities in the HTTP Server, including a severe vulnerability that could potentially lead to remot...
item.dc:creator: The Hacker News

item.title: DAEMON Tools Supply Chain Attack Compromises Official Installers with Malware
item.description: A newly identified supply chain attack targeting DAEMON Tools software has compromised its installers to serve a malicious payload, according to findings from Kaspersky. "These installers are distribu...
item.dc:creator: The Hacker News

item.title: Microsoft Edge Stores Passwords in Process Memory, Posing Enterprise Risk
item.description: A proof-of-concept exploit (PoC) shows how someone with admin privileges can exploit the issue to steal passwords, and thus use them to engage in further malicious activity....
item.dc:creator: Dark Reading

item.title: FTC to ban data broker Kochava from selling Americans’ location data
item.description: The FTC will ban data broker Kochava and its subsidiary, Collective Data Solutions (CDS), from selling location data without consumers' explicit consent to settle charges alleging that it sold precise...
item.dc:creator: Bleeping Computer

item.title: China-Linked UAT-8302 Targets Governments Using Shared APT Malware Across Regions
item.description: A sophisticated China-nexus advanced persistent threat (APT) group has been attributed to attacks targeting government entities in South America since at least late 2024 and government agencies in sou...
item.dc:creator: The Hacker News

item.title: The EOL Blind Spot in Your CVE Feed: What SCA Tools Miss
item.description: Critical vulnerabilities can exist in open source software your scanners don't check. HeroDevs reveals how EOL software creates blind spots in CVE feeds and SCA tools, and how you can receive a free e...
item.dc:creator: Bleeping Computer

item.title: Vimeo data breach exposes personal information of 119,000 people
item.description: The ShinyHunters extortion gang stole personal information belonging to over 119,000 people after hacking the Vimeo online video platform in April, according to data breach notification service Have I...
item.dc:creator: Bleeping Computer
